_note: Human-editable source of truth (spec Phase 4.1). Generated from the persona corpora, the live platform-review run's journey/screenshot evidence (runs/8112a07d-.../<persona>/journey.json), and the gtm_cs persona doc's cited business facts. Riché edits this file directly going forward; the app never mutates it.

features.name: Scorecard letter grade + factor breakdown
features.description: The A-F letter grade and its underlying factor scores for a company's own or a vendor's rating.
features.rationale: The single most-cited value across all three personas — instantly board-legible for CISOs, the anchor for VRM triage, and the GTM demo moment. Checked daily by power users.

features.name: Score issues / findings list
features.description: The list of externally-visible findings driving a scorecard, filterable by severity/category.
features.rationale: Necessary and used daily, but the deliverable's #1 dislike ('factor drill-downs lack prioritization') shows the value is buried — users can't tell which 3 issues to fix first without manual triage.

features.name: Attribution dispute / correction flow
features.description: The path to dispute a misattributed finding (wrong IP/asset) and request re-verification.
features.rationale: Low day-to-day reach (only touched when a dispute arises) but disproportionately high-stakes — the CISO and VRM persona docs both name unresolved disputes and SLA misses as top churn triggers.
features.evidence: personas/ciso/persona.md Red Flags: 'A score drop caused by the platform's own misattribution'; personas/vrm/persona.md Top Pain Points

features.name: Financial impact / risk quantification
features.description: Dollars-at-risk framing of security posture for board and cyber-insurance conversations.
features.rationale: A real differentiator (board-credible dollar framing is rare in the category) but the run's own journey note flags uncertainty over whether it reads as 'board-credible ... or a black box' — adoption lags the capability.

features.name: Report Center / board & QBR exports
features.description: Exportable, brandable reports for board packets and customer QBRs.
features.rationale: Independently visited by both the CISO and GTM/CS personas in the same run — it serves the board-reporting job AND the CSM's QBR-export job, a rare dual-persona anchor feature.

features.name: Alerting / rule builder
features.description: Configurable rules controlling which score/finding changes trigger a notification.
features.rationale: Essential for noise control, but the journey's own framing question — 'would this create alert fatigue' — plus the VRM pain point 'alert noise: score-change notifications without materiality context' shows real usage lags the configuration surface's potential.

features.name: Attack Surface Intelligence
features.description: Asset discovery and attribution feeding the outside-in rating.
features.rationale: The accuracy of this surface is existential for the small set of power users (security/VRM analysts) who audit attribution weekly, even though most users never look at it directly — its failures are what surface as disputes elsewhere.

features.name: Vendor portfolio management
features.description: Tiering, saved views, and bulk triage across the full third-party vendor book.
features.rationale: The VRM persona's core weekly loop and its top-rated like ('Portfolio view supports fast vendor triage') — this is the feature the VRM's whole day-to-day depends on.
features.evidence: runs/8112a07d-.../vrm/portfolios.jpg; runs/8112a07d-.../vrm/my-vendors.jpg; deliverable like 'Portfolio-scale vendor triage'

features.name: Automatic Vendor Detection (4th-party)
features.description: Automated discovery of a vendor's own vendors (concentration/4th-party risk).
features.rationale: The journey's own framing — 'signal or noise?' — plus the fact this ships as an upsell module means most portfolios haven't turned it on yet, even though 4th-party blindness is a named VRM pain point it directly answers.

features.name: Questionnaires (Atlas)
features.description: Send/track vendor due-diligence questionnaires (SIG Lite/Core, custom).
features.rationale: Necessary for diligence but the deliverable's #2 dislike ('Questionnaires feel siloed from ratings') means the value is hidden behind a manual-correlation workflow, not surfaced automatically.

features.name: Remediation action plans
features.description: SLA-tracked remediation collaboration with vendors, with closure evidence.
features.rationale: Heavily used by the subset of VRM power users actively chasing overdue-critical vendors, but invisible to the majority of the portfolio at any given time — a classic power-user-critical, low-reach surface.

features.name: Requests log / audit trail
features.description: Chronological log of vendor requests and responses for exam evidence.
features.rationale: Opened almost exclusively during an exam or audit prep, then untouched for months — a compliance checkbox with structurally low reach that's a natural CCB kill-or-fold-in candidate.

features.name: Prospect scorecard (sales demo)
features.description: Live lookup of any company's scorecard, used as the signature sales-demo moment.
features.rationale: The deliverable's top GTM like — 'Instant prospect scorecards' — a zero-setup value demo that shortens every sales cycle it's used in.

features.name: Vendor engagement / invite flows
features.description: What a customer's own vendors see: invites, collaboration requests, response tracking.
features.rationale: Drives the collaboration loop VRM depends on, but the persona doc's own red flag — vendors 'reporting the invite/collaboration flow is confusing or feels like a sales funnel' — shows the value is undercut by friction on the receiving end.

features.name: Marketplace / integrations
features.description: Pre-built integrations (Jira, SIEM/GRC, ticketing) into a customer's existing stack.
features.rationale: The journey's framing question — 'how easily does this land inside a customer's existing stack' — plus both CISO and VRM persona docs naming 'APIs into my SIEM/GRC' as a want-not-have shows integration depth is still an adoption gap, not a shipped strength.

features.name: Verified Assessments (Early Access)
features.description: A newly-introduced Early Access surface for pre-verified vendor assessments.
features.rationale: Explicitly an Early Access surface as of this run — by definition too new to have meaningful adoption yet; the open question is discoverability and demo confidence, not fit.

features.name: Notification center
features.description: The in-app feed of score-change, dispute, and system notifications.
features.rationale: The journey's own framing — 'would a customer see value here weekly, or churn-inducing noise?' — combined with alert-fatigue complaints elsewhere suggests this surface has drifted from useful to ignored, a candidate to fold into a smarter alerting redesign rather than maintain as-is.

features.name: MAX managed services (SCDR workflows)
features.surface: n/a — services-delivered, not a self-serve UI surface
features.description: Managed remediation/response workflows delivered as a service on top of the platform.
features.rationale: Named in the GTM/CS persona doc as the company's fastest-growing product line (370% YoY) — the clearest expansion-revenue growth engine in the portfolio, even though it's services-heavy rather than a pure self-serve feature.
features.evidence: personas/gtm_cs/persona.md: 'SCDR, MAX managed services — the company's fastest-growing product at 370% YoY'

features.name: AI product-ops workflows (this app)
features.surface: n/a — internal product-ops tooling, not a customer-facing SSC surface
features.description: The persona-evaluation, reviewer/approval, ticket-drafting, and Friday-Update AI workflows built in this project.
features.rationale: Freshly built and in active weekly use this cycle (every phase's review gate + the matrix→Linear pipeline) — the AI Containment Rate metric tracks exactly this surface's trustworthiness as it scales.
features.evidence: spec.md Phases 1-3; this repository's own commit history
